habits: Collagen 15g + vitamin C, 30–60 min before iso / plyo / Nordic work — feeds the tendons
Carbs around sessions: fist+ before, refill after. Up on strength & speed days, down on recovery days
2.5–3L water; electrolytes on speed & sweat days
Omega-3: oily fish 2×/wk or ~2g EPA+DHA — connective-tissue recovery
3+ colors of fruit & veg daily — micronutrients that build collagen
Casein or Greek yogurt before bed on training days
around_training: Before (45–60 min): easy carbs (fruit, oats, rice) + 15g collagen + vitamin C — primes tendons on iso, plyo & Nordic days
During: water + electrolytes; sip carbs only if the session runs past ~75 min
After (within 60 min): 35–40g protein + a fist or two of carbs to refill
Strength & speed days → carbs up (~330g). Recovery days → carbs down (~220g). Protein stays ~150g every day
Splits & mobility: go in warm and hydrated — collagen pre-session helps the stretch build tissue, not just tolerance
playbook: Anchor every meal: a palm-to-two-palms of protein (chicken, steak, fish, eggs)
Add a fist of carbs (rice, potato, bread) — double it on strength & speed days
Half the plate vegetables or fruit whenever available
Breakfast that builds tissue: eggs + Greek yogurt + oats + fruit
Pocket snacks: Greek yogurt, jerky, protein shake, nuts, fruit
Alcohol: skip it or cap at one — it taxes sleep and recovery most
note: Home base-block fueling, periodized to the plan: ~2,800 kcal on training days (carbs up), ~2,400 on recovery days (carbs down); protein steady at ~150g (2.0–2.2 g/kg per ISSN). Collagen + vitamin C before elastic/tendon work is the lever for 'tight, fast tendons'. No caffeine after 2pm — sleep is where tendons and speed are built. Full-time targets (2,900–3,200) kick in Aug 15. Calibrate by the scale + morning energy, not one day.
